step_by_step_thinking:
The myosin head detaches from actin during the power stroke after it has completed its contraction cycle. This process involves several steps, including binding of ATP to the myosin head which triggers conformational changes that allow for release from the actin filament. 

My answer is C: Binding of ATP to head To provide further context:

When ATP binds to the myosin head, it destabilizes the cross-bridge between the myosin and actin filaments, leading to their separation as part of muscle relaxation or recovery phase post-contractile activity. Therefore, option C accurately describes this mechanism.
raw_response:
The myosin head detaches from actin during the power stroke after it has completed its contraction cycle. This process involves several steps, including binding of ATP to the myosin head which triggers conformational changes that allow for release from the actin filament. 

My answer is C: Binding of ATP to head To provide further context:

When ATP binds to the myosin head, it destabilizes the cross-bridge between the myosin and actin filaments, leading to their separation as part of muscle relaxation or recovery phase post-contractile activity. Therefore, option C accurately describes this mechanism. Answer: C (Binding of ATP to head).
